Transcrição Completa: Supercharged Entrepreneur with Mark Dhamma
[00:00]
"Thank you everybody. Um, appreciate it and appreciate you Jeremy for inviting me back out again and everybody else. Appreciate you being cool with me coming back. I'm coming with some totally different content this time around."
[00:15]
"Um, and I actually have um I actually have a confession to make early on. So, I've been coaching entrepreneurs for 15 years. I started in 2008. I think that's 15 years ago. And back then I my background is in sport science and physiology. So I used to train athletes and then I just happened to take get a lot of clients who are entrepreneurs."
[00:35]
"So I started to focus on them and I took it really really seriously at first. For the first 10 years I I was all about that. How do I help how do I help entrepreneurs perform at the absolute best? And my confession is and then I actually took a break."
[00:50]
"I started working with my wife and we have a hair business. It's called Swirly Curly Hair. It's um hair products for women. Hair products and education for women with afro-type hair. And at the time when Jeremy was introduced to me by Jay, actually me and Jay go a long way back."
[01:10]
"Jay introduced Jeremy to me and that's how we got working. I wasn't coaching anymore. I wasn't coaching entrepreneurs. I was just focusing on building this business with my wife. Jeremy came and while I was working with Jeremy, like I said, I I actually kind of semi-retired for a bit while I was working with him."
[01:30]
"And then I was semi-retired just working with clients, word of mouth, people would find out about me. Um, all the way up until April, actually. So the last five years I've not really, this is my confession, I've not really been taking my work as a high performance coach to entrepreneurs that seriously."
[01:50]
"Kind of got lazy with it. I actually had three kids in that time. Um, I started to get a bit of a dad bod for me. Me and Brandon go way back, too. And I used to kind of look like Brandon back in the day. Maybe a bit better than Brandon. Uh but but since then I got a but dad abroad."
[02:10]
"I got lazy. I wasn't keeping up with the research. I was focusing on building the business with my wife and you know I was doing some things for myself to optimize myself but I wasn't really it wasn't my main thing right and that's changed that changed in April where I was like you know what this is what I well first of all my wife kicked me out of our business."
[02:30]
"So that that's what really happened. Like my wife hated working with me. we got two different visions and different workers. She hated working with me. She'd been trying to kick me out for the last 5 years. Um, and she successfully did it back in April."
[02:45]
"And it's then where I was like, you know what? I'm getting back into coaching and I'm giving it my very best effort. And the reason I felt good coming into coaching entrepreneurs and giving my very best effort is because I believe entrepreneurs are the change makers of the world."
[03:00]
"We're the ones who are going to solve the world's problems. So, I wouldn't really leave it up to governments, wouldn't leave it up to anyone else. It's entrepreneurs. We We're the ones who find the problems and come up with solutions. We're the risktakers."
[03:15]
"And with that, I'm really proud to dedicate my life to helping entrepreneurs like you be at your very best. Because when you're at your best, it affects your organization and it affects the customers. Affects everybody you're in contact with."
[03:30]
"Whether it's your products, your services, who you work with, it affects them. And I believe that has a ripple effect. So today, with that said, that's my confession. I've been kind of lazy, guys. I'm kind of slacking. But today, um, this is my way of changing that. I want to tell you what's really up."
[03:50]
"Okay, so let's go. Anyone seen this yet?"
[03:53]
"David Beckham."
[03:55]
"You seen it yet?"
[03:56]
"The first episode."
[03:58]
"This first episode, anyone seen it? So, um, David Beckham's a hero of mine. I grew up in Manchester, England. I was born in 1985, Manchester, England."
[04:10]
"My, um, when I was a kid, my pajamas were Manchester United pajamas. My bedding was Manchester United bedding. My wallpaper was Manchester United wallpaper. The only pictures I had on my wall were framed photos of the Manchester United team and the players, individual players."
[04:30]
"That's like what it's like in Manchester growing up. My dad was a semi-professional soccer player. My uncle um was actually a um a scout and he had a long career with um Manchester City football club."
[04:45]
"My my uncle actually went on to be the head coach of Anguila soccer team, the car Caribbean country, Anguila, their soccer team. So it's like really embedded in like what we do. Okay. have brainwashed Manchester United."
[05:00]
"So, I actually remember Beckham's first game. I was watching it on the TV with my dad. I remember his first game when he first came on. And Beckham, he he scored an amazing goal on his first his debut and everyone was like, 'Wow, this guy's awesome.'"
[05:15]
"And then me and my friends at school, we all tried to bend it like Beckham, of course, the very next day. Anyway, I bring it up because during that time in the 90s to well, it was like 93 to 2013, Manchester United was the most dominant team in England."
[05:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Manchester United Dominance Era"
NÚMEROS: 1993-2013 (20 anos de dominância)
VISUAL: Imagem de Sir Alex Ferguson e David Beckham, logotipo do Manchester United, representação visual da era dourada do clube
[05:40]
"They dominated the Premier League. The Premier League is known to be, and I would say it absolutely is, the most competitive sports league in the world. It's the most competitive sports league in the world. There's the most money in that league and the most competition."
[06:00]
"Every one of those teams are top quality teams. And it's you're in Europe, it's different than in the US. In the US, in the US, if you lose your games, like if you're in the MLS and you lose every single game, you're still in the MLS the next year, right? Same with NFL and all that."
[06:15]
"You're going to, right, Brett? It's the same in England. If you lose all your games or if you're at the bottom of the table at the end, you're out of the Premier League. You're out. You're in a low division."
[06:30]
"That means your revenue is I don't know how much it's cut by, but it's almost completely gone. You're out. You know, the money's in the Premier League and then the more money is in the Champions League. That's another thing."
[06:45]
"So there's fierce competition to one stay in the Premier League, to win the Premier League, get into the Champions League where there's more money and to absolutely not get out of the Premier League because you are losing money if you do that."
[07:00]
"So with that said, fierce competition, we as Manchester United, we dominated for years. And a big part of it was thanks to this coach, Sir Alex Ferguson."
[07:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sir Alex Ferguson Era"
NÚMEROS: 1986-2013 (27 anos como técnico)
VISUAL: Foto de Sir Alex Ferguson, linha do tempo mostrando seu período como técnico do Manchester United, troféus e conquistas
[07:15]
"He signed in 1986, a year before I was born. um retired in 2013. So all my younger years, this guy was our coach and we dominated."
[07:30]
"Now, this is all going to make sense to you and what it applies to entrepreneurship in a moment, but what I have to say is that there's a secret to Manchester United success that not many people outside of Manchester, my hometown, actually know about."
[07:45]
"It's known back where I grew up because everyone's Manchester. We we know everything that's going on in soccer. My best friend, his dad was the um accountant for the the supertore where they sell all the shirts and everything."
[08:00]
"We everybody knows everything what's going on, right? And there's a secret. We had a secret. And that secret allowed us to out compete all the other teams for close to 20 years when then all the other teams caught up."
[08:15]
"I'm going to share that secret in a moment. what that was. But first, a little side note, a funny fact. So, last night I came in. Jeremy was kind enough to let me stay here and I got here around 9:00 p.m."
[08:30]
"and uh Jay and Jeremy were here playing uh Madden on the on the TV. We were playing Madden and um I was saying last time I played Madden was like 2007 or something when I was in college."
[08:45]
"That's how I actually learned about American football playing Madden when I was back in the UK playing college. Anyway, did you guys know that one of the first American football games was in 1875 and it was Harvard versus Yale?"
[09:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Harvard vs Yale - The Coaching Advantage"
NÚMEROS: 1875 (primeiro jogo), 30 anos de dominância Yale, apenas 6 vitórias Harvard
VISUAL: Logos de Harvard e Yale, gráfico comparativo mostrando resultados dos jogos, ícone de treinador vs sem treinador
[09:05]
"That was one of the very first American football games. What's interesting is that for the next 30 years, Yale won the majority of the games with Harvard only winning uh six in 30 years."
[09:20]
"The difference was as soon as they started playing the games, as soon it was announced, Yale hired a coach. They had a coach coach the football team. Harvard didn't they didn't hire a coach. And for the next 30 years, Yale won. Harvard only won six."
[09:35]
"And then Harvard hired a coach. And then from then on, pretty much every college started hiring coaches. And that's how it really got proliferated sports coaches in the United States. It's pretty interesting."
[09:50]
"So, here's the secret. We had a secret. And that secret was that early on, Sir Alex Ferguson, our coach, brought in sports, science, and nutrition into Manchester United. Sport science and nutrition. Nobody was doing that before."
[10:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Manchester United's Secret Weapon: Sports Science & Nutrition"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Ícones representando ciência esportiva e nutrição, comparação entre abordagem tradicional vs científica, imagem de laboratório/nutrição
[10:10]
"nutrition for professional soccer players around that time was a chippy like I don't know if any of you guys know what chip is bag of French fries um French fries beer pints of beer um just whatever random English food you'd eat it wasn't it didn't exist they were just eating whatever"
[10:30]
"it was very common for professional soccer players to drink lots of alcohol especially after games. It there was no awareness at all around eating right or you know drinking good or optimizing your sleep. None. There was no awareness whatsoever. It just didn't exist."
[10:50]
"All they focused on was the skills. Skills, soccer skills, passing, dribbling, shooting. The training they had lots of training. The training was generally let's run them till they're dead. Keep sprinting, running, push harder. No thought on recovery."
[11:10]
"No, no thought on recovery. No thought on like, okay, we're going to sprint for this. There was zero science behind it. It was just push them till they drop and then they're not fit enough, so push them harder next session to get them fit."
[11:25]
"It's kind of like well it's kind of like I would argue entrepreneurship today. Entrepreneurship today. The culture of entrepreneurs has to be focused on skills, knowledge. Focus on knowledge and skills. Knowledge and skills. Knowledge and skills."
[11:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hustle Culture in Entrepreneurship"

[11:45]

[12:00]
"and then now nowadays it's a level playing field there. They're looking for other advantages which some of them will go into today what they are."
[12:10]
"So I said this last time and I'm saying it again. I really encourage you to think of yourselves as decision-making athletes. Decision-making athlete."
[12:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Think of Yourself as a Decision-Making Athlete"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Ícone de atleta + ícone de cérebro/decisão, conceito visual de empreendedor como atleta de tomada de decisões
[12:25]
"Can't read this, but as an entrepreneur, you face countless decisions that can make or break your success. Just like an athlete, your ability to make strategic, calculated choices is crucial."
[12:40]
"By seeing yourself as a decision-making athlete, you'll start to make better decisions around your recovery, around your well-being, and essentially you make better decisions because that's what you do."
[12:55]
"You have to make great strategic decisions. And if you're tired, you're worn out, you're stressed, you're not going to do the best decisions. And it's not your fault. It's not your fault."
[13:10]
"You just, we've, and I'll include myself, we've just inherited a hustle culture. That's what entrepreneurs have done. They've had to hustle. They've had to push. And it's not surprising. It's because like no one helps entrepreneurs. You can't We're not being helped."
[13:25]
"You know, government don't help us. Make things harder. Um, if we go to college, they're not going to teach you how to you take care of yourself and make sure you perform at your best. As an entrepreneur, that doesn't exist."
[13:40]
"If you was an athlete in college, you're going to get all the help you need in to recover and to take care of yourself. They're going to give you all that education, but not as an entrepreneur."
[13:55]
"Entrepreneurs, you're just left to figure it out for yourselves and spend your own money on shit too, to figure it out for yourselves. So, it's not it's not your fault. It's just what we've inherited. But things are changing."

It's not I'm not going to go too deep, but we we basically have five types of brain wave patterns, okay?"
[24:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Five Brainwave Patterns"
NÚMEROS: 5 tipos de ondas cerebrais
VISUAL: Gráfico de ondas cerebrais mostrando Beta, Alpha, Theta, Delta e Gamma, cada uma com características visuais distintas
[24:35]
"One of them is beta. And beta is kind of the everyday thinking. Your everyday state is mostly beta, okay? It's concentration, arousal, alertness. But if you have too high levels of beta, which most entrepreneurs do, then you're going to experience anxiety, um, depression, feelings of separation."
[24:55]
"It's kind of like you're in the fight or flight anxiety stage. Okay. The next one is alpha state. And alpha is a very useful state for entrepreneurs who wants to do some who wants to get focus and create things and do stuff."
[25:10]
"That's why you have neutropics like alpha brain because it's trying to get you into an alpha state. Alpha state is a associated with flow. Getting into flow, relaxation, super learning, relaxed focus, light trans, increased serotonin production."
[25:30]
"Theta is like when you're in um a dreamless when you've got REM sleep, you know, when you're having light sleep, you're in you're in REM, your body is actually paralyzed. You could force it if you needed to, but you're kind of in a paralyzed state."
[25:45]
"Theta is the best for hypnotherapy. So everything you learned this morning, if you're going to do um some hypnotherapy or hypnosis by going through that in a theta state, that's where you want to be. That's what gets to your subconscious mind."
[26:00]
"Delta, dreamless sleep. Um and then gamma gamma waves are the fastest brain waves. These are the ones that we actually know the least about. Um if your brain produces high levels of gamma waves, you tend to be happier and more receptive."
[26:15]
"It's associated with higher levels of IQ and um concentration. It's also associated with more spiritual states. Usually when people have a spiritual experience, they're actually in a gamma state."
[26:30]
"Now, the reason why I'm sharing this with you is because a big part of brain optimization is being able to shift the states, shift your brain states to what you want to actually accomplish. And that's what we're going to talk about right now."
[26:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "PEMF Therapy - Pulse Electromagnetic Field Therapy"

[26:50]
"So, here is um pulse electromagnetic field therapy, PEMF it's called. And these devices push your brain into different states within seconds. So, if you wanted to be really really focused and get some really thinking work done, maybe writing, some creative work, or you just needed to get something done, maybe shooting videos or whatever,"
[27:10]
"you can use this device and it will push you into the alpha state and you'll just feel it. Same with theta. If you wanted to do some um hypnosis or some visualization, you wanted to get clear on your goals, clear on your dreams, theta, you can hit a button and it'll push you into that theta state."
[27:30]
"These are safe. They well they well used. The great thing about P EMF, it also has the ability to literally increase the number of mitochondria in your cells, specifically your brain cells. These devices you can put around your head. It'll increase the number of mitochondria."
[27:50]
"The more mitochondria you get, the more energy it produces. More energy in your brain means more willpower, more ability to focus, less distractability. Um, more energy, yeah, available to brain, more focus, and better decision making."
[28:05]
"You are decision making athlete. You want a lot of energy in your brain. That's one way of doing it."
[28:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Binaural Beats - Brainwave Entrainment"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Imagem humorística de pessoa com fones de ouvido, representação de ondas sonoras binaurais
[28:20]
"Binaural beats. I thought this was a funny picture, so I put it on. But most people have heard of binaural beats. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. But brainwave entrainment using binaural beats. They also help improve your focus, cognitive performance, and learning speed."
[28:40]
"If you're listening to binaural beats that can shift you into a delta state, for example, or even an alpha, but merely a delta state while you're reading or listening to an audio, well, not listening to an audio, but while you're reading, you're going to retain that a lot faster."
[28:55]
"you're going to retain the information much better. And as an entrepreneur, one of my mentors was Tai Lopez. I got to work with Tai for some years. That's how I met Brandon Carter with Tai. That's almost 10 years ago now. That's where I first met Brandon."
[29:10]
"Um Tai's a well-known entrepreneur. That's why I bring him up. But that guy is a learning machine. He is just learning, learning, learning. Read. He does read a book a day. It's not shit And you'll find that the best entrepreneurs like yourselves, they're always learning."
[29:30]
"They're reading more books, you're learn getting more skills, coming to places like this. You're constantly learning. So the more you can learn and retain, the more of an advantage you have of other entrepreneurs."

[30:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "QEEG Brain Mapping and Neurofeedback"
NÚMEROS: 20 minutos de teste, 7 anos atrás (experiência pessoal)
VISUAL: Imagem de pessoa com eletrodos na cabeça, mapeamento cerebral colorido, representação de neurofeedback
[30:25]
"QEG brain mapping and neuro feedback. Now we're going a little deep. Everyone's heard of the other stuff. So QG brain mapping. Um, basically you can go to a center. I went to one about 7 years ago when I was back in Los Angeles. I went to Peak Brain Institute"
[30:45]
"and what they'll do is they'll put a bunch of electrodes on your head, have you sit down and like run you through a few tests for about 20 minutes and then the computer spits out images and it can see what exactly is going on in your brain."
[31:00]
"It's relevant because it can detect abnormalities or imbalances. If you have an abnormal abnormality or imbalance then it can give you ADHD like symptoms and it can make you more anxiety, give you more anxious. It can make you less able to focus. It can make you slur your words a little bit."
[31:20]
"And the thing is pretty much everybody has abnormalities or imbalances because the way you get abnormalities or imbalances is by doing too much multitasking. watching TV with a lot of jump cuts, um traveling, lack of sleep, um stimulants, um other lifestyle factors, poor sleep habits, exposure to toxins."
[31:40]
"So once you have your brain mapped out, then you can start doing things like neuro feedback training to actually get your brain to work in an optimal way. There's loads of neuro feedback training um on the market. It kind of like meditation on steroids."
[32:00]
"Um, some of them are games where might be on your phone or on a computer and you'll wire up your head to a um a monitoring device and you have to use your brain to move things. Okay. Has anyone heard of muse? Muse?"
[32:15]
"Yeah, muse is a a type of neuro feedback. There's lots of different types. Muse is a type. Focus calm is a type. Focus calm is really good because it teaches you to be able to make a lot of decisions while staying very, very calm and relaxed. Great for trading neuro feedback. Great for trading all that stuff."
[32:35]
"Um, there's many different types of neuro feedback. What you don't want to do is just start experimenting on your own if you don't know what you're doing because you can actually rewire your brain in the wrong way. Okay? So, be very careful with that."
[32:50]
"Other things that it can do more focus, attention, give you more drive and a lot more self-awareness. So that's neuro feedback and Q EG brain mapping."
[33:05]
"I'm sharing these things for you because in the athletic world it's all about optimizing your physical capabilities. In our world it's all about optimizing your cognitive capabilities."

[38:15]

[38:35]
"and there's this beautiful um hotel resort place um right by where Trump's Golf Course is in Palos Verdes in Los Angeles. And we we went there. It was like our anniversary or something. We stayed for a night."
[38:50]
"And when we got there, um, we had a it was like 500 bucks or 700 bucks for the room. And when we got there, they were like, 'Okay, it's $40 to park your car.' I was like, 'What? Just like paid you 700 bucks or whatever to get a room. It's another $40.'"
[39:10]
"That's what I was thinking. And just as it was happening, I just my mind was like, in my mind, I didn't say any. I was like, 'I don't want to pay for that.' And then I kid you not, someone just came over, had a whispered in their ear, and walked off."
[39:25]
"and she's like, 'Oh, you know what? This one's covered. This one's on us. You don't need to pay for it.' I was, 'Okay.' Later on that day, um I was walking around and the um I don't know why, but I had a craving for gelato. I never have cravings for gelato."
[39:45]
"I was like, you know, I'd really like a gelato. It's our anniversary, you know, walk around a corner, there's a damn gelato place there, like like a pop-up gelato place. 📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
15 anos de experiência em coaching (desde 2008)
1985 - Ano de nascimento (Manchester, Inglaterra)
1986-2013 - Era de Sir Alex Ferguson no Manchester United (27 anos)
1875 - Primeiro jogo Harvard vs Yale
30 anos de dominância Yale (Harvard ganhou apenas 6 jogos)
1993-2013 - Período de dominância do Manchester United (20 anos)
3-4 horas de treino zona 2 recomendado
4 minutos de treino Tabata/HIIT
20 minutos, 2x por dia, 3 meses - Protocolo HeartMath
15-20 minutos - Sessão de fotobiomodulação pré-performance
7 anos atrás - Experiência com QEEG brain mapping
$40 - Estacionamento do hotel (história de manifestação)
$500-700 - Custo do quarto do hotel
8-9 cifras - Resultado de Alex Becker
$110 milhões - Referência de venda de negócio
2009 - Início no marketing digital
5 tipos de ondas cerebrais (Beta, Alpha, Theta, Delta, Gamma)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Fundamentos de Performance do Empreendedor:
Tomada de decisão baseada em dados (wearables, Oura Ring)
Testes laboratoriais (parasitas, toxinas, hormônios, sensibilidades alimentares, deficiências nutricionais)
Protocolo nutricional personalizado (calorias, dieta anti-inflamatória, suplementação baseada em exames)
Planejamento estratégico (OKRs do Google/LinkedIn, One Thing Thinking, CEO Reflective Journaling, Modelo GROW)
Protocolo de otimização do sono (horários consistentes, antes das 22h, terapia de luz, luz vermelha, aumento de oxigênio, santuário do sono)
Exercício para energia cerebral (zona 2, Tabata/HIIT, treino de força)
Nootrópicos (creatina, racetams, nicotina, bacopa, rhodiola)
Hipnoterapia e visualização para mindset
Próxima Fronteira - Otimização Cerebral:
PEMF Therapy (Pulse Electromagnetic Field) - Mudança de estados cerebrais em segundos
Binaural Beats - Aprendizado acelerado e retenção (Brain.fm recomendado)
QEEG Brain Mapping + Neurofeedback (Peak Brain Institute, Muse, FocusCalm)
Fotobiomodulação - Luz vermelha intranasal (usado por Steph Curry)
Dual N-Back Training - Melhoria cognitiva em semanas
Heart-Brain Coherence - HeartMath device, técnicas de Dr. Joe Dispenza
ESTRUTURA:
Analogia Central:
Empreendedores = Atletas de Tomada de Decisão
Manchester United = Pioneiros em ciência esportiva e nutrição (vantagem competitiva)
Cultura Hustle tradicional vs Otimização científica
Yale vs Harvard = Importância do coaching desde 1875
Hierarquia de Performance:
Fundamentos (já conhecidos pela maioria dos empreendedores sérios)
Otimização Cerebral (próxima fronteira - onde poucos estão)
Aspectos metafísicos (intuição, manifestação, espiritualidade)
5 Padrões de Ondas Cerebrais:
Beta: Estado cotidiano (concentração, alerta, mas excesso causa ansiedade)
Alpha: Flow state (foco relaxado, super aprendizado, criatividade)
Theta: Sono REM (hipnoterapia, subconsciente, visualização)
Delta: Sono profundo sem sonhos
Gamma: Ondas mais rápidas (QI elevado, experiências espirituais, felicidade)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Não aplicável diretamente (apresentação focada em performance, não em métricas financeiras específicas)
Referência indireta: Alex Becker atribui sucesso de 8-9 cifras à otimização corporal/mental, não a estratégias de anúncios
Implicação: Investimento em coaching/otimização = ROI exponencial em tomada de decisões e resultados empresariais
INSIGHTS PRINCIPAIS:
Empreendedores estão 20 anos atrás dos atletas em otimização de performance
A maioria ainda opera em "hustle culture" (conhecimento + habilidades + café)
Poucos aplicam ciência para otimização cerebral/cognitiva
Vantagem competitiva está migrando de "trabalhar mais duro" para "otimizar melhor"
Performance não é apenas física/mental, mas também espiritual (manifestação, intuição)
DURAÇÃO: ~48 minutos FORMATO: Palestra presencial com slides, exemplos pessoais, Q&A ao final
